pages.text:
Friday Sept 2nd. 4.15. Simon, Tinder + Eve set out. 5.30. We reached Eckington Mill camp-site. There's a padlock to get in the gate, 1812. Court End Farm Campsite [?arrow/note]

We read + made up the bed + cycled to Eckington Manor for supper. Gorgeous meal. The man near us was a scientist next to Toys 9 us at Junet 2 - the longest [?for] Sainsbury factory making chemical stuff, 1860 phosphate + onwards. He lives in Worcs. Fat tummy. We both saw the Kingfisher - lovely pitch on a lake [?river]

__Saturday__
We walked off across the 2 rivers - Tindered. Be let off on the island. Looked at Strensham Church. A lot of family memorials, brasses covered because of bats, Norman tub font, saints' paintings (16). Cavalier man with moustache + wife. (We walked across M5 service) It rained + rained. We picked lots of blackberries. We could see the Malverns, the Cotswolds + Bredon.

Lunch in van.
In the afternoon we read + picked more blackberries. I've never picked so many. A brilliant year for blackberries. As we got

back in the van it started to rain again.

__Sunday__
We swept the floor + set off for Groome Park. Sign on one of village driveways:
"Harley parking only
Jap bikes will be crushed."

Groome Park has the old building, __Groome Court__, which we did not see inside. It had a temporary exhibition in it. Grayson Perry. We walked in the huge grounds, in part of which we could let Tinder off the lead. We saw the pavillion + where Hare Krishna people were singing. We had lunch in the canteen (but outside) part of the old RAF hospital, [?airfield] [?Defford]. saw the RAF museum including a Canberra cockpit where the Bombaimers lay on the floor. There is a fabulous walled garden owned by a man who made his money putting up stages for rock concerts, from Bham. He + his wife have been restoring it - very impressive.

We got home at 4 pm.
